title: penstemon glaber var. wardii
intro: Penstemon glaber var. wardii, also known as Beardtongue, is a plant species in the mint family, characterized by its white, pink, or purple flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Penstemon glaber var. wardii include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing coughs and sore throats. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
